description: Portuguese exhibits extensive vowel reduction in unstressed syllables, where /a/, /e/, /o/ are often realized as [ɐ], [i], [u] respectively. This process is particularly pronounced in European Portuguese and affects both lexical and grammatical words, contributing to the characteristic vowel reduction patterns of the language.
sources: Mateus, M. H., & d'Andrade, E. (2000). _The Phonology of Portuguese_. Oxford University Press.
Schilling, E. T. (2013). _The Sound of Brazilian Portuguese: A Phonological and Phonetic Description_. De Gruyter Mouton.
Barbosa, P. A. (2010). _The phonetics and phonology of Portuguese vowel reduction_. In _Studies in the Phonology of Portuguese_ (pp. 15-45).
